data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T3 (Integration feasibility, overlay) — CLEAN, no findings.

T3 is an overlay that fires only when the plan integrates an external API/CLI/service/library or asserts a never-before-used capability. This close-out ticket integrates nothing external: its scope is emptying a ruff `per-file-ignores` allowlist, re-validating `# noqa: BLE001`/`# noqa: T201` reasons and deleting dead annotations, documenting the error-handling convention in `CLAUDE.md`/a contributing doc, and a final `make lint` + full-test-suite + joe-janitor-audit gate. All named tools/commands are internal and already used elsewhere in this epic — not-applicable.

AFFIRMATIONS:
- (a) technical_feasibility — achievable as described: emptying allowlist entries, re-checking inline noqa reasons, writing docs, and running an existing lint/test/audit gate are well-trodden, in-repo operations; no capability gap.
- (b) named subcommands/endpoints exist — MATCH (internal): `make lint`, the test suite, and the joe-janitor audit are pre-existing project tooling already invoked by the sibling sweep batches; ruff `per-file-ignores` is the established mechanism the plan tightens. No external CLI/API subcommand or endpoint is asserted.
- (c) auth/HTTPS preconditions — N/A: no network, API, or service call is introduced in this chunk.
- (d) SPIKE routing — N/A: no critical external-capability gap exists to spike.

T3 PASS as not-applicable — all integrations are internal/already-used; asserting a mismatch here would be a false positive.
